Hard corals with big rings and fleshy tissue on Singapore shores: how to tell them apart

wildsingapore homepage

wildfactsheets homepage
wild shores of singapore blog

all animals | all plants | concepts | glossary | search

cnidarians text index | photo index Phylum Cnidaria **Hard corals with small ringsHow to tell them apart?updated Nov 11**

Some corals with small ring-shaped corallites may look similar. Here’s more on how to tell them apart.


Trumpet coral
Colony appears boulder-shaped,
but is not solid.


Corallites large trumpet-shaped, long narrow flaring to oval opening.


Moon coral
Colony encrusting to boulder-shaped.


Zebra coral
Colony encrusting,
sometimes small boulder-shaped.


Meteor coralColony boulder-shaped,
sometimes in hillocks.


Corallites neat domes with small opening and fine neat radiating lines.


Corallites circular ring with thick white radiating walls on a black background.


Corallites a tiny circular ring often not packed tightly, with tiny bumps between the corallites.

More about identifiying coral species.
